operationsProcessActivities.name: Planning
operationsProcessActivities.description: The art and science of understanding a situation, envisioning a desired future, and laying out effective ways of bringing that future about. In the operations process, planning produces understanding, develops a plan or order, and prepares the force.

operationsProcessActivities.name: Preparing
operationsProcessActivities.description: Activities performed by units and Soldiers to improve their ability to execute an operation: rehearsals, plan refinement, task organization changes, integration of new units, pre-combat checks and inspections, sustainment preparation, and movement to assembly areas.
typicalArtifacts: Updated sustainment posture (basic loads, supply requests)

operationsProcessActivities.name: Executing
operationsProcessActivities.description: Putting a plan into action by applying combat power to accomplish the mission. Commanders make decisions to seize, retain, and exploit the initiative; staffs synchronize warfighting functions; subordinates execute tasks.
typicalArtifacts: Decision point matrices and synchronization matrices in execution

operationsProcessActivities.name: Assessing
operationsProcessActivities.description: Determination of the progress toward accomplishing a task, creating a condition, or achieving an objective. Continuous activity that occurs throughout the operations process. Outputs feed re-planning and the targeting cycle.
typicalArtifacts: MOEs and MOPs with current values
Combat assessment products (BDA, MEA, reattack recommendations)
